channel.title: Unity on paper.archive
channel.description: Recent content in Unity on paper.archive

item.title: Using Visual Studio Code as the default editor for Unity3D on Mac
item.description:
When developing with Unity on Mac you usually code with MonoDevelop. But it doesn’t support Korean, which was really annoying.

MS recently released Visual Studio Code for Mac and it supposedly supports Unity, so I gave it a try.

Install

  1. First install VS Code https://code.visualstudio.com/

  2. Press Cmd+P and install the extension with ext install Omnisharp might not even be needed…

  3. Install mono from the Mac terminal with brew install mono takes a while
